title: Suspected Hamas member detained in Cyprus over weapons procurement
plaintext:
Cypriot authorities have detained a suspected member of Palestinian militant group Hamas wanted in Germany for procuring weapons and ammunition for attacks on Israeli or Jewish facilities, German federal prosecutors said on Tuesday.

The Lebanese-born suspect, identified only as Kamel M. in line with German privacy rules, was detained at Cyprus' Larnaca airport on March 6, arriving from Lebanon, they added in a statement.

The suspect is wanted in relation to the transport of 300 rounds of live ammunition, according to prosecutors. It wasn't clear from the statement where the rounds had come from, or where they were thought to be heading.

"The operation served as preparation for deadly Hamas attacks on Israeli or Jewish institutions in Germany and Europe," they said.

Police also searched the suspect's apartment in Berlin.

Once Kamel M. is extradited to Germany, a judge will decide on pre-trial detention, the statement said.

Attacks against Jews and Jewish targets have risen worldwide since Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza, triggered by the militant group's 2023 attacks on Israel.

(Reporting by Thomas Seythal; Editing by Sharon Singleton)
sentence: Police also searched the suspect's apartment in Berlin.
context: Police also searched the suspect 's apartment IB .

title: PICTURES: Colour flies as Joburg celebrates Holi festival
plaintext:
Hundreds of guests attended the Holi festival celebration at the Shree Radhe Shyam Mandir in Marlboro.

The vibrant Holi festival, held in Marlboro, saw families, friends and community members come together to mark the Hindu spring festival known for its celebration of colour, joy and renewal.

Attendees tossed bright powdered colours into the air and onto one another, filling the temple grounds with clouds of pink, yellow, blue and green while music and laughter echoed through the venue.

Holi is traditionally celebrated across India and by Hindu communities around the world to welcome the arrival of spring and symbolise the triumph of good over evil.

The festival also represents a time for forgiveness, unity and the renewal of relationships.

The event was organised by India Club South Africa in association with the High Commission of India and the Consulate General of India.

Organisers said the celebration aimed to share Indian culture with the broader community while strengthening cultural ties in South Africa.

Guests enjoyed traditional food, music and dance performances throughout the day, with many describing the colourful celebration as a highlight on Johannesburg’s cultural calendar.
sentence: The event was organised by India Club South Africa in association with the High Commission of India and the Consulate General of India.
context: The event was organised by India Club South Africa IA with the High Commission of India and the Consulate General of India .

title: Campbelltown TAFE locked down during major police operation along Narellan Rd
plaintext:
A major police operation is unfolding at a TAFE campus in an outer southwestern Sydney suburb amid reports of a suspicious individual being spotted.

In a post on their official X and Facebook pages, NSW Police confirmed an operation is underway at an education facility on Narellan Road in Campbelltown.

“The public are urged to avoid the area and follow all police directions,” the notice reads.

NewsWire understands it relates to reports of a suspicious individual being spotted on the campus.

More to come.
sentence: In a post on their official X and Facebook pages, NSW Police confirmed an operation is underway at an education facility on Narellan Road in Campbelltown.
context: In a post on TO X and Facebook pages , NSW Police confirmed an operation is underway at an education facility on Narellan Road in Campbelltown .

title: Sri Lanka name Gary Kirsten as head coach with 2027 World Cup in focus
plaintext:
By Pearl Josephine Nazare

Sri Lanka have appointed South African Gary Kirsten as their new head coach on a two-year contract as they begin preparations for the 2027 One-Day International World Cup, the country’s cricket board said on Monday.

Kirsten, 58, previously guided India to the 2011 World Cup title and later coached South Africa from 2011 to 2013. He also briefly served as Pakistan’s white-ball coach in 2024, resigning six months into the job.

He replaces Sanath Jayasuriya, who stepped down after Sri Lanka’s disappointing campaign at the recent Twenty20 World Cup.

“Sri Lanka Cricket wishes to announce the appointment of former South African cricketer Gary Kirsten as the new head coach of the national men’s team, effective from April 15, 2026,” the board said.

“The appointment of the new head coach is part of Sri Lanka Cricket’s efforts to revamp the structure of the National High Performance Centre.”

The 2027 World Cup will be hosted by South Africa, Zimbabwe and Namibia.

The Proteas and Zimbabwe will qualify automatically for the tournament as hosts. Namibia, despite co-hosting, will have to go through the regular qualification pathway because they are not a full International Cricket Council member.
sentence: Namibia, despite co-hosting, will have to go through the regular qualification pathway because they are not a full International Cricket Council member.
context: Namibia , DC - hosting , will have to go through the regular qualification pathway because they are not a full International Cricket Council member .

title: Police car crashes into Western Sydney home for the second time
plaintext:
For the second time in two years, a police car has crashed into a family home in Western Sydney, leaving it in ruins.

CCTV footage captured the car speeding across the street in Merrylands, before zooming over a speed bump and ploughing into the house.

The vehicle is captured making heavy impact with the garage, causing it to partially collapse.

Smoke is then seen billowing from the front of the car as its headlights remained flashing.

According to police, the vehicle was responding to an urgent job, but was not involved in a pursuit.

No other vehicles were involved in the incident and no other injuries were reported

However, due to the damage the residents of the home were forced to stay elsewhere for the night.

It follows an incident in 2024 where a police car crashed into the property’s front fence, but with much lighter damages.

An investigation into the incident is underway.
sentence: CCTV footage captured the car speeding across the street in Merrylands, before zooming over a speed bump and ploughing into the house.
context: CCTV footage captured the CS across the street in Merrylands , before zooming over a speed bump and ploughing into the house .

title: Prepare now for the next crisis
plaintext:
From oil shocks to load shedding, crises force change, and rising global tensions could again reshape South Africa's energy and transport choices.

The Arab-Israeli War in 1973 was a turning point for the world… but not in the way many would think.

The subsequent oil crisis – as members of the Organisation of Oil Producing Exporters froze supplies in solidarity with those fighting Israel – led to a revolution in the car industry.

Fuel-efficient cars were suddenly in demand – and given that many of them came from Japan, that country jumped multiple rungs up on the industrialisation prosperity ladder.

Ordinary consumers realised the days of gas-guzzling automobiles were over.

In SA, we’ve already had a wake-up call with electricity: thanks to load shedding, solar systems have proliferated.

Now, with water supplies being untrustworthy, many are moving to boreholes and storage tanks.

We wonder if the latest Middle East crisis might change the way we view our transport options.

Fuel is set to soar in price, even if there is no outright shortage – although that is a real, worrying possibility.

Moving to smaller, less thirsty cars will help – and so will maximising the use of those cars through lift clubs, for example.

The bottom line is nothing is unlimited and we, as a country, are vulnerable in many areas.

So, the watchword is: be prepared.
sentence: From oil shocks to load shedding, crises force change, and rising global tensions could again reshape South Africa's energy and transport choices.
context: From oil shocks to load shedding , crises force change , and rising global tensions could again reshape South Africa 's EA transport choices .

title: Doorbell camera captures violent rollover crash that trapped 2 people on residential O.C. street
plaintext:
A neighbor’s doorbell camera captured the moment a car slammed into a pick-up truck on a residential street in Fullerton, flipping it on its side and trapping two people inside on Sunday evening.

The Fullerton Police Department responded to the crash at the intersection of Valencia Drive and Courtney Avenue just before 6:30 p.m. They cut off the windshield of the truck to extricate the two passengers trapped inside, said Fullerton Fire Capt. Robert Thompson.

The neighbor’s video shows a dark blue Toyota sedan moving quickly down Valencia Drive as a silver Ford pickup truck makes a slow right turn from Courtney Avenue onto Valencia Drive. The sedan crashes into the truck, momentarily sending it into the air and then knocking it over on its side. The crash caused severe front end damage to the sedan and activated its airbags, video from news-gathering agency OnScene shows.

The violent collision along the quiet street drew a crowd of onlookers, who gathered around as the passengers, a man and a woman, were loaded onto wheeled stretchers and into ambulances.

The passengers and the driver of the sedan were all transported to hospitals with minor injuries and complaints of pain, according to Fullerton Police Department spokesperson Kristy Wells. Police confirmed to The Times that the incident was not DUI-related.

Wells said the department had not made any arrests in the crash and did not anticipate doing so.
sentence: A neighbor’s doorbell camera captured the moment a car slammed into a pick-up truck on a residential street in Fullerton, flipping it on its side and trapping two people inside on Sunday evening.
context: A neighbor ’s doorbell camera captured the moment a car slammed into a pick - up truck on a residential street in Fullerton , flipping it on IS and trapping two people inside on Sunday evening .

title: Syrian President says armed forces strengthened along borders, supports Lebanon and Iraq’s measures
plaintext:
Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa said on Monday that Syria has coordinated a unified stance with regional countries and reinforced its defensive forces along the borders as a precautionary measure to prevent the conflict from spilling into Syrian territory.

He added that Syria supports the serious steps being taken by the governments of Iraq and Lebanon to keep dangers away from their countries and prevent any escalation toward conflict.

The president also affirmed Syria’s backing of Lebanon’s efforts, led by President Joseph Aoun, to disarm Hezbollah.
sentence: The president also affirmed Syria’s backing of Lebanon’s efforts, led by President Joseph Aoun, to disarm Hezbollah.
context: The president AA Syria ’s backing of Lebanon ’s efforts , led by President Joseph Aoun , to disarm Hezbollah .

title: Trump to hold press conference after US markets close
plaintext:
US President Donald Trump says he will hold a press conference later in the day before returning to Washington DC from Florida but gave no details about what he would discuss.

The press conference is scheduled for 5.30pm on Monday, after the US markets close.

It comes as global markets slumped more than a week into the war on Iran amid soaring oil prices and fears of spiking inflation.

"Many important meetings and phone calls taking place today," Trump added in the social media post announcing his plans to speak to reporters after attending a Republican fundraiser.
sentence: The press conference is scheduled for 5.30pm on Monday, after the US markets close.
context: The press CI scheduled for 5.30pm on Monday , after the US markets close .

title: Winner of Netflix’s ‘Outlast’ arrested on suspicion of child rape, sexual battery charges
plaintext:
An inaugural winner of Netflix’s “Outlast” survival competition series is facing charges for alleged child rape and sexual battery.

Paul Preece Jr., one of the three men who secured victory in the series’ debut season in 2023, was arrested Friday in Knox County, Tenn. The Knox County Sheriff’s Office database shows Preece was arrested on suspicion of aggravated sexual battery, rape of a child and attempted rape of a child. Preece, 51, did not immediately respond Monday to a request for comment.

The Knox County Sheriff’s Office set Preece’s bond for $150,000. He is due to appear in court on Tuesday for his arraignment.

When “Outlast” debuted in 2023, Preece was one of 16 contestants battling harsh Alaskan winter elements for a grand prize of $1 million. Netflix’s fan site, Tudum, described the Tennessee-based Preece as a longtime hunter and fisherman who said he “passes on his love for the outdoors by teaching skills to youth and novice hunters each season.” He said at the time he felt his childhood struggles with homelessness in eastern Kentucky informed his survival capabilities.

“I believe Alaska will throw everything she has at me, but I’ve been unknowingly preparing for this all my life,” he said.

Preece and fellow contestants Nick Radner and Seth Lueker emerged victorious in the Season 1 finale, splitting the grand prize. “Outlast” returned for its second season in 2024 and was renewed in February 2025 for a third.
sentence: He said at the time he felt his childhood struggles with homelessness in eastern Kentucky informed his survival capabilities.
context: He said at the TH felt his childhood struggles with homelessness in eastern Kentucky informed his survival capabilities .

title: Chiefs, Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III agree to deal as NFL free agency begins: reports
plaintext:
The Kansas City Chiefs reportedly made one of the first big splashes of NFL free agency.

The Chiefs and Super Bowl MVP running back Kenneth Walker III agreed to a deal on Monday, according to multiple reports. Walker joining the Chiefs would certainly bolster their chances of getting back to the playoffs following a 6-11 season.

Walker had a breakout year in 2025 as he helped guide the Seattle Seahawks to their second Super Bowl title. He ran for 1,027 yards and five touchdowns as he and Zach Charbonnet dominated the backfield and bolstered Sam Darnold’s passing game.

In Super Bowl LX, Walker helped Seattle push past the New England Patriots. He ran for 135 yards and had two touchdown catches in the 29-13 win. He was the first running back to receive the honor since Terrell Davis did it for the Denver Broncos in Super Bowl XXXII.

Kansas City could use the help in the running back region. Isiah Pacheco, Kareem Hunt, Clyde Edwards-Helaire and Damien Williams have all been serviceable in years past but injuries marred the team’s success in that area.

The Chiefs were 25th in rushing yards during the 2025 season.

As of now, Charbonnet will be the starting running back with George Holani and Kenny McIntosh on the depth chart.
sentence: He ran for 135 yards and had two touchdown catches in the 29-13 win.
context: He RF 135 yards and had two touchdown catches in the 29 - 13 win .

title: Lebanon’s information minister: Government working to protect vital infrastructure and ensure supplies
plaintext:
Paul Morcos, Lebanon’s information minister, said the country’s leadership is working daily to protect vital infrastructure and prevent escalation amid ongoing tensions.

Speaking to reporters before entering a legislative session at the Lebanese parliament, Morcos said that Nawaf Salam and the government, in coordination with the president, are making continuous efforts to safeguard key facilities and keep them away from the risk of war.

He noted that recent strikes did not target vital infrastructure, though he stressed that Lebanon cannot rely on any guarantees from the Israeli side.

Morcos also revealed that ministers hold daily morning meetings chaired by Salam to coordinate work among ministries and respond to citizens’ needs, particularly in relief efforts and supply distribution.

The meetings also focus on monitoring the impact of rising global oil prices and their effects on Lebanon’s local market.

Morcos added that the government is working to ensure the continued availability of essential living, medical, and food supplies, while facilitating the delivery of shipments to different regions in line with directives from the president and the prime minister.
sentence: Paul Morcos, Lebanon’s information minister, said the country’s leadership is working daily to protect vital infrastructure and prevent escalation amid ongoing tensions.
context: Paul Morcos , Lebanon ’s information minister , said the country ’s leadership is working daily to protect vital infrastructure AP escalation amid ongoing tensions .

title: Two alleged murder and attempted murder suspects dead in Inanda police shootout
plaintext:
Two suspects allegedly linked to several cases of murder and attempted murder were shot dead in a shootout with police in Inanda on Monday.

Provincial spokesperson Col Robert Netshiunda said police were searching for at least five suspects, and during an operation which started just after midnight on Monday, two of the suspects were located in the Mzomusha area with the assistance of a drone.

“When police approached the house in which the suspects were hiding, a shootout ensued which resulted in the two suspects, aged 20 and 25, sustaining fatal gunshot wounds.”

Two firearms were found in their possession. A search for the remaining suspects is ongoing.
sentence: Provincial spokesperson Col Robert Netshiunda said police were searching for at least five suspects, and during an operation which started just after midnight on Monday, two of the suspects were located in the Mzomusha area with the assistance of a drone.
context: Provincial spokesperson Col Robert Netshiunda said police were searching for at least FS , and during an operation which started just after midnight on Monday , two of the suspects were located in the Mzomusha area with the assistance of a drone .

title: No fuel concerns among State resources majors ‘at this stage’, says WA’s mining lobby CME
plaintext:
WA’s well-stocked miners have not revealed any immediate signs of concern about access to diesel supplies that power scores of rail and haulage fleets, but are staying alert to how conflict in the Middle East plays out.

As of Monday there were no reports of disruption to operations or concerns about fuel supplies, according to Chamber of Minerals and Energy WA, which counts some of the State’s largest resources companies as members.

It comes as petrol and diesel prices soar across Australia with oil topping its highest since 2022 as concerns for global supply spreads.

Iron ore majors are understood to keep a close eye on fuel stocks as a matter of course and have their own supplier contracts in place. Australia relies largely on diesel imports from Asia.

“Member companies have not reported disruptions to their operations or concerns about the availability of fuel at this stage,” Chamber of Minerals and Energy WA chief Aaron Morey on Monday said.

“The conflict in the Middle East is evolving and its impact on supply chains is being monitored closely.”

A Fortescue spokeswoman said the Pilbara miner actively managed supply chain risks across its operations “including fuel supply”.

“We maintain appropriate fuel stocks and have contingency arrangements in place to support the continuity of our operations, which continue as normal.”

BHP, Rio Tinto and Fortescue are all trialling methods to diversify fuel sources for haul trucks and locomotives away from diesel.
sentence: “The conflict in the Middle East is evolving and its impact on supply chains is being monitored closely.”
context: “ The conflict in the Middle East is evolving and its impact on supply CI being monitored closely . ”

title: Mpumalanga suspect arrested for kidnapping, raping and murdering seven-year-old girl
plaintext:
Police in Mpumalanga have arrested a 21-year-old man for allegedly kidnapping, raping, and murdering a seven-year-old girl in Charl Cilliers on Sunday.

The girl’s body was found by police in a mealie field after she was reported missing and a search was conducted.

According to Mpumalanga police spokesperson Col Mavela Masondo, police received a complaint about a missing child and immediately activated police units, including crime intelligence, K9 and detectives, to search for the child.

He said the team was joined by private security officers, farmers and members of the local community policing forum.

“The search led to the discovery of the child’s clothes dumped near the mealie field. Further investigation resulted in the arrest of the suspect, who led police to the mealie field where the child’s body was found,” Masondo said.

The suspect is expected to appear in the Secunda magistrate’s court on Tuesday.
sentence: According to Mpumalanga police spokesperson Col Mavela Masondo, police received a complaint about a missing child and immediately activated police units, including crime intelligence, K9 and detectives, to search for the child.
context: According to Mpumalanga police spokesperson Col Mavela Masondo , police received a complaint about a missing child and immediately activated police units , including CI , K9 and detectives , to search for the child .

title: Welcome to SA’s dry… humour
plaintext:
This airport water shortage was, apparently, not some act of God or an Iranian missile.

Greetings tourists! Welcome to OR Tambo International Airport – gateway to our wonderful country and mirror of some of the wondrous things you may experience here.

To put you in a South African mood, you will see there is no water in any of our terminals. It’s much the same outside in our suburbs.

We’ve had plenty of rain this year, but that doesn’t translate into water coming out of our taps (or faucets as you may know them).

This airport water shortage was, apparently, not some act of God or an Iranian missile – this was general South African efficiency.

Our Airports Company South Africa (Acsa) decided to embark on some “maintenance” to its water supply facilities.

Seemingly all at the same time, without warning and without telling the restaurants, never mind the bathroom facilities.

So, you won’t get a coffee – which is a good thing, because then you won’t have to go to the loo.

And that’s another thing about us in South Africa – we tend to look on the bright side of our not infrequent disasters.

It’s a great pity OR Tambo International doesn’t have a firepool you could rinse off in… you’ll have to get someone to explain that one to you.
sentence: This airport water shortage was, apparently, not some act of God or an Iranian missile – this was general South African efficiency.
context: This airport water shortage was , apparently , not some act of God or an Iranian missile – this was general South AE .

title: James Slater: Man, 31, charged with murder after Strathtulloh fatal shooting
plaintext:
A man charged after a fatal shooting has remained expressionless as he was told he’d remain in custody for more than four months.

James Slater, 31, appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates’ Court on Monday morning for a brief filing hearing as his case was formally lodged with the court.

Wearing a black jumper, Mr Slater did not appear to react as he was remanded into custody until July by magistrate Steven Raleigh.

The Werribee man was arrested in Melbourne’s southeast on Sunday night following a shooting three days earlier in the city’s outer west.

Police say they located a 36-year-old man with critical injuries about 12.40am on March 5 near the intersection of the Snipe Drive and Spadefoot Place in Strathtulloh.

He died at the scene despite efforts from emergency services.

At the time, Victoria Police alleged two men, the 36-year-old and a 63-year-old, had attended the suburb before an argument broke out with a third male, Mr Slater, who is believed to be known to the pair.

Several shots were allegedly discharged and the third male fled the scene.

According to police, Mr Slater was arrested on Sunday evening in Skye and charged with murder later that night.

In court, his lawyer noted the accused man had been seen by a “forensic officer” and was aware he could not apply for bail in the Magistrates’ Court.

Mr Raleigh remanded him in custody to return to court on July 29.
sentence: Wearing a black jumper, Mr Slater did not appear to react as he was remanded into custody until July by magistrate Steven Raleigh.
context: Wearing a black jumper , Mr Slater did not AT react as he was remanded into custody until July by magistrate Steven Raleigh .

title: Rihanna's Beverly Hills home targeted in brazen daylight shooting
plaintext:
A woman fired shots into Rihanna's Beverly Hills mansion on Sunday afternoon, Fox News Digital confirmed.

Officers responded to a "ADW (assault with a deadly weapon) shots fired" call in the affluent area of Los Angeles at around 1:15 p.m., an LAPD spokesperson told Fox News Digital.

"Approximately 10 shots heard," according to LAPD audio dispatch, who also described the shots being fired "from across the street."

Rihanna, whose full name is Robyn Rihanna Fenty, was reportedly home when the suspect fired multiple rounds from inside a vehicle, according to the Los Angeles Times.

The suspect was located shortly after and "taken into custody without further incident," an LAPD spokesperson told Fox News Digital.

No injuries were reported, and one weapon was recovered at the scene.

Rihanna's representatives did not immediately respond to Fox News Digital's request for comment.

Rihanna and her partner, musician A$AP Rocky, welcomed a baby boy in May 2022. She gave birth to their second son in August 2023.

In July 2022, she was named the youngest self-made female billionaire by Forbes when she hit the $1.4 billion net worth marker, earned by her music, beauty and fashion ventures.

Rihanna owns 50% of Fenty Beauty, and a 30% stake in her Savage X Fenty lingerie brand. Her stake in the beauty brand alone was valued at an estimated $700 million.
sentence: Officers responded to a "ADW (assault with a deadly weapon) shots fired" call in the affluent area of Los Angeles at around 1:15 p.m., an LAPD spokesperson told Fox News Digital.
context: Officers responded to a " ADW ( assault with a deadly weapon ) shots fired " call in the affluent area OL Angeles at around 1:15 p.m. , an LAPD spokesperson told Fox News Digital .

title: Murder charge after man dies following a one punch attack
plaintext:
A man has been charged with murder following an alleged fatal one-punch attack.

Police allege a 62-year-old man was knocked unconscious when he was assaulted, fell and hit his head on the pavement along Hay St in Perth’s CBD about 11.05pm on Friday.

Paramedics rushed the man to hospital with critical injuries, but he later died.

A 37-year-old Scarborough man is accused of striking the victim and stealing property from him before he fled.

Police allege the Scarborough man was also involved in another incident and stole property from a 41-year-old man about three hours earlier.

The accused man has been charged with murder, grievous bodily harm, aggravated robbery, stealing and gaining benefit by fraud.

He has been remanded in custody and is due to appear before the Perth Magistrates Court on March 27.

The charge comes weeks after Brodie Dewar, 20, was charged with manslaughter following an alleged one-punch attack on Labor figure Tim Picton.

Mr Picton was allegedly knocked unconscious in a one-punch attack outside a Perth nightclub in the early hours of December 27 last year. He died from his injuries in hospital about three weeks later.

Mr Dewar was also charged with causing bodily harm over a similar alleged one-punch attack that occurred outside a Kalamunda pub days earlier.

Mr Dewar has been granted bail while his matter progresses through the courts.
sentence: Mr Dewar has been granted bail while his matter progresses through the courts.
context: Mr Dewar has been granted BW his matter progresses through the courts .

title: Lebanese health ministry says death toll from Israeli strikes up to 394
plaintext:
Israeli strikes on Lebanon have killed 394 people in the past week, including 83 children and 42 women, the country's health minister said Sunday.

Rakan Nassereddine also said at a press conference that nine rescue workers were among the dead, condemning attacks on medical teams and ambulances.

A previous toll announced on Saturday by the minister had put the number of dead at 294 since Lebanon was drawn into the regional war last Monday between Iran, Israel and the United States.
sentence: Rakan Nassereddine also said at a press conference that nine rescue workers were among the dead, condemning attacks on medical teams and ambulances.
context: Rakan Nassereddine also said at a press conference that nine rescue WW among the dead , condemning attacks on medical teams and ambulances .

title: South Korean in sight of victory at Blue Bay LPGA event
plaintext:
South Korea's Mi Hyang Lee has extended her Blue Bay LPGA lead to three strokes at Hainan Island in China after a topsy-turvy one-under 71 featuring seven birdies and six bogeys.

Chasing her third LPGA Tour title at the Jian Lake Blue Bay Golf Course and her first since 2017, the 32-year-old enters Sunday's final round at 12 under.

Trailing Lee at nine-under are countrywoman Hye-Jin Choi (68 on Saturday) and China's Yu Liu (73).

Defending champion Rio Takeda (67) of Japan, India's Aditi Ashok (70) and South Korea's A Lim Kim (71) are tied for fourth at eight-under.

"A lot of up and down, but still finished under par, so really looking forward to tomorrow," said Lee, who is battling through some shoulder pain this week.

"Last night I couldn't sleep without my medication, so hopefully just one more day for tomorrow," she said.

Robyn Choi, the 27-year-old Gold Coast player who's Australia's sole representative in the tournament, had a level-par round to sit on three under for the tournament, tied for 16th place.
sentence: "Last night I couldn't sleep without my medication, so hopefully just one more day for tomorrow," she said.
context: " Last night I could n't sleep without my medication , SH just one more day for tomorrow , " she said .

title: Israeli military issues urgent evacuation warning to residents south of Litani River
plaintext:
The Israeli military issued an urgent warning to residents of South Lebanon who are located south of the Litani River, calling on them to evacuate immediately.

In a post on X, the Israeli army spokesperson, Avichay Adaree, said that what it described as "Hezbollah's terrorist activities" are forcing its forces to operate with strength in the area.

The army said airstrikes are ongoing and that it is conducting large-scale operations in the region. It urged residents to leave their homes immediately and move north of the Litani River to ensure their safety.

The warning stressed that anyone staying near members of Hezbollah, its facilities, or its military assets is putting their life at risk.

It further cautioned that remaining south of the Litani River could endanger residents and their families, and reiterated its call for immediate relocation northward.

The statement also warned that any movement southward could expose civilians to danger.
sentence: In a post on X, the Israeli army spokesperson, Avichay Adaree, said that what it described as "Hezbollah's terrorist activities" are forcing its forces to operate with strength in the area.
context: In a post on X , the Israeli army spokesperson , Avichay Adaree , said that what it described as " Hezbollah 's terrorist activities " are forcing its forces to operate WS in the area .

title: Popular stretchy toys urgently pulled from shelves over asbestos fears
plaintext:
A range of popular stretchy kids’ toys have been urgently pulled from shelves after fears the figures may contain asbestos.

The Stretcherz Stretch Squad XXL and Slammerz stretchy toys were urgenbttly recalled by the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) amid fears the sand inside may contain asbestos.

“The sand inside the figures may cause a risk to health if the product is damaged and the sand is released, as tremolite asbestos has been detected in some samples after laboratory testing,” the recall notice read.

The toys were sold by HTI Toys HK Limited and were available for purchase online and in-store nationwide between August 25, 2025 and February 11, 2026.

Customers who have the toys have been told to stop using the toys immediately and contact the retailer for a full refund.
sentence: The Stretcherz Stretch Squad XXL and Slammerz stretchy toys were urgenbttly recalled by the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) amid fears the sand inside may contain asbestos.
context: The Stretcherz Stretch Squad XXL and Slammerz stretchy toys were urgenbttly recalled by the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission ( ACCC ) amid fears the sand inside may CA .

title: Sake Mash Brewed Aboard ISS Returns to Japan; Final Product Already Sold for ¥100 Million
plaintext:
OSAKA — A batch of moromi fermentation sake mash brewed aboard the International Space Station arrived in Japan on Friday.

The moromi, which was created in the Kibo Japanese experiment module aboard the ISS, arrived in the evening at Kansai Airport from Los Angeles following its return to Earth on Feb. 27.

Alcohol was reportedly detected in the mash, and its maker, Dassai Inc. of Iwakuni, Yamaguchi Prefecture, now aims to turn it into refined sake.

The company, which paid a fee to use Kibo, aims to usher in a future where people can enjoy sake even after moving to the moon.

The project began in October 2025, when a brewing device containing rice, koji mold and yeast was launched via rocket from the Tanegashima Space Center. Once aboard Kibo, astronauts began the brewing process.

The mash was kept frozen until it splashed down off the coast of Los Angeles, where it was recovered and then transported to Japan.

According to the company, the mash is expected to yield about 100 milliliters of refined sake, which has already been sold for about ¥100 million. The product will be delivered to the buyer once the brewing process is complete.

“We are now standing at the starting line for making sake on the moon,” Dassai Chairman Hiroshi Sakurai said. “Sake will surely even be able to enrich lives in outer space.”
sentence: The company, which paid a fee to use Kibo, aims to usher in a future where people can enjoy sake even after moving to the moon.
context: The company , which paid a fee to use Kibo , aims TU in a future where people can enjoy sake even after moving to the moon .

title: Flash floods in Nairobi kill 10, disrupt flights at major airport
plaintext:
Aid workers pulled bodies from floodwaters across Nairobi on Saturday after overnight flash floods killed at least 10 people, swept away dozens of cars and disrupted flights at East Africa’s biggest airport, authorities said.

In the industrial neighbourhood of Grogan, security guard John Lomayan, 34, looked at the body of an elderly man he recognised — a roadside egg seller — trapped beneath a car that had been washed away when the Nairobi River burst its banks.

“I saw him being carried by the water from up there,” he said, gesturing up the road. “We didn’t know where he had gone. It is only now that we see him under the car”.

A Reuters reporter saw three bodies pulled from underneath cars. Police said 10 people had been confirmed dead so far.

Scientists say global warming is worsening floods and droughts across East Africa by concentrating rainfall into shorter, more intense bursts. A 2024 World Weather Attribution study found climate change had made devastating rains in the region twice as likely as before.

Kenya Airways said the rains had disrupted flights to Nairobi and forced some to divert to the coastal city of Mombasa.

“So many cars, so much stuff, I don’t know. Everything was just (washed away). All of the water (came) ... from that river,” shocked resident Cedric Mwanza said, referring to the Nairobi River.

Reuters
sentence: Aid workers pulled bodies from floodwaters across Nairobi on Saturday after overnight flash floods killed at least 10 people, swept away dozens of cars and disrupted flights at East Africa’s biggest airport, authorities said.
context: Aid workers pulled bodies from floodwaters across NO Saturday after overnight flash floods killed at least 10 people , swept away dozens of cars and disrupted flights at East Africa ’s biggest airport , authorities said .

title: Gnowangerup: WA’s earthquake town cops another swarm
plaintext:
The small town of Gnowangerup, about four hours south east of Perth, has been shaken by a swarm of small earthquakes.

Tremors measuring around magnitude 3 were recorded at about 2.20pm and 4.30pm on Friday, striking just a few kilometres northeast of the town.

Another quake hit the same area on Saturday morning, measuring magnitude 2.7.

The Geoscience website reports 31 earthquakes of varying magnitude within the last week — and more than 70 since February.

The strongest was a magnitude 3.8 which hit around 15km north of Gnowangerup — with eight people reported having felt it.

Gnowangerup 24/7 Fuel Bowsers owner Ron Bett said small earthquake swarms were not unusual for the region.

“It’s not uncommon for us and you get a bit of rattling in your house,” he said.

“With these recent ones, I don’t know of anyone who’s had damage. Sometimes you don’t even feel them.

“I’d say this week alone I’ve heard people mention around ten of them.”

Mr Bett said earthquakes have been stronger in the past.

“About two and a half years ago we had a pretty bad one,” he said.

“A friend’s farmhouse was so badly damaged they had to move out entirely.”
sentence: Fuel Bowsers owner Ron Bett said small earthquake swarms were not unusual for the region.
context: Fuel Bowsers owner RB said small earthquake swarms were not unusual for the region .

title: GFL looking to break long drought at 2026 carnival
plaintext:
A general bye applies in the Goldfields Football League on July 11, when the men’s team chases an elusive division one crown at the annual Nutrien Ag Solutions Country Championships in Perth.

The 2026 series will take place from July 9-12, when the GFL looks to improve on two wins at each of the past two carnivals as the team’s division one championship drought moves into its 34th year.

Under Bill Hansen, Goldfields last year grabbed a second win on the final day in a come-from-behind result against Avon by a point, 6.6 (42) to 6.5 (41).

They trailed Avon by 23 points midway through the second half but kicked the last four goals of the match to seal third place on percentage.

The GFL season opens on April 11, when Mines Rovers launch their flag defence at Digger Daws Oval against Kalgoorlie.

The Diorites open the season in pursuit of back-to-back premierships for the first time since the club’s historic four-peat of 2007-10.

Also on April 11, Kambalda host last year’s grand finalists Boulder at Kambalda West Oval to complete round one.

The Eagles, under new coach Stan Ward, will play just two of their first five games on their home ground.

However, they have drawn four consecutive home games from rounds seven to 11 — broken up by a club bye on May 30 in round eight.

Kambalda also play each of their last three games of the qualifiers at home.
sentence: They trailed Avon by 23 points midway through the second half but kicked the last four goals of the match to seal third place on percentage.
context: They trailed Avon by 23 points midway through the second half but kicked the LF goals of the match to seal third place on percentage .

title: Japan Approves World’s 1st iPS Cell-based Treatment; May Be Available as Early as Summer
plaintext:
Two regenerative medicine products made from induced pluripotent stem cells have been conditionally approved, the Health, Labor and Welfare Ministry announced Friday. The approval comes with certain conditions and time limits.

This opens the door to the world’s first commercialized treatment using iPS cells. Following national deliberations, a decision will be made on the price and whether it will be covered by public health insurance. The treatment could be available as early as this summer.

iPS cells can transform into various cell types and are created by introducing several genes into cells, such as those from the skin or blood.

Cuorips Inc., a startup out of the University of Osaka, has created a product called “RiHEART” that targets severe heart failure caused by ischemic cardiomyopathy. Cardiomyocytes made from iPS cells are processed into sheets. Three sheets are attached to the heart to promote blood vessel regeneration and restore cardiac function. Sales are expected to begin around this autumn.

Amchepry, a product from Sumitomo Pharma Co., targets Parkinson’s disease. Transplanted nerve cells made from iPS cells into the patient’s brain produce dopamine, which is expected to improve motor disorders such as tremors in the limbs and difficulty in walking. The company aims to start selling the product between summer and autumn this year.
sentence: Following national deliberations, a decision will be made on the price and whether it will be covered by public health insurance.
context: Following national deliberations , a decision will be made on the price AW it will be covered by public health insurance .

title: Israeli media: Hezbollah plans attacks from Syria as Syrian president seeks to strike its positions along Bekaa Valley border
plaintext:
According to Israeli public broadcaster citing intelligence sources, Hezbollah is reportedly planning to launch attacks on Israel from Syrian territory.

The report added that Syrian President Ahmed al-Sharaa intends to target Hezbollah positions along the border in the Bekaa Valley region.
sentence: According to Israeli public broadcaster citing intelligence sources, Hezbollah is reportedly planning to launch attacks on Israel from Syrian territory.
context: According to Israeli public broadcaster citing intelligence sources , Hezbollah is reportedly PT launch attacks on Israel from Syrian territory .

title: Santos boss Kevin Gallagher pockets $5.6m in share sale
plaintext:
Santos chief executive Kevin Gallagher has pocketed $5.6 million in his first share selldown in more than two years.

Filings on Friday show Mr Gallagher sold 830,132 shares on-market at an average of $6.77 apiece on February 27, “primarily to fund pending personal tax obligations”.

The Santos chief and his family hold another $10.2m of ordinary shares based on Friday’s closing price of $7.46, while he is also sitting on another 1.99 million of rights and restricted shares.

The stock holding includes a $4.7m payment of performance rights for the 2025 financial year under a golden handcuffs agreement struck five years ago.

The personal windfall was confirmed two weeks ago when Santos used its annual results to reveal plans to further cut costs by jettisoning 10 per cent of its 4000-strong workforce.

It also announced a strategic review of its Australian oil and gas business, which includes assets in WA, NSW, Queensland and South Australia.

Santos shares have added more than 10 per cent since the US and Israeli attacks on Iran on the weekend sent oil and gas prices surging.
sentence: Santos chief executive Kevin Gallagher has pocketed $5.6 million in his first share selldown in more than two years.
context: Santos chief executive Kevin Gallagher has pocketed $ 5.6 million in his first share selldown IM than two years .

title: ‘It’s going to change lives’: Samora Mangesi hosts new pension-focused talk show
plaintext:
Media personality Samora Mangesi has scored a new gig hosting SABC1’s latest intervention talk show, Future Proof.

The show, in partnership with the Government Employee Pension Fund (GEPF), premieres on Monday and is set to simplify pension fund jargon into clear, digestible language.

The show informs, educates and empowers members, reinforces trust in pension systems and highlights the practical value of financial preparedness and informed decision-making.

“I am deeply honoured to be a part of conversations that help South Africans plan better for their golden years,” Samora said.

“Over the years I have had the pleasure to entertain and as we enter this chapter I get to use my voice to inform and educate South Africans. I look forward to seeing the impact that this show is going to have and how it is going to help change lives.”
sentence: Media personality Samora Mangesi has scored a new gig hosting SABC1’s latest intervention talk show, Future Proof.
context: Media personality Samora Mangesi has scored a new gig hosting SABC1 ’s LI talk show , Future Proof .

title: Young girl left with serious facial injuries after dog attack in coastal Perth suburb
plaintext:
A young girl has suffered major facial injuries after she was mauled by a dog in Perth’s northern suburbs.

The girl, who is under the age of 10, was attacked outside a home in Hillary, a coastal suburb located in the metropolitan area of Perth.

St John WA were called to the scene around 3:40pm on Wednesday, March 4 before she was rushed to Perth Children’s Hospital.

A hospital spokeswoman said the girl is in stable condition.

The City of Joondalup, the local government area where Hillary is located, said it “takes all dog attack report seriously and is investigating the incident as a matter of priority”.

“The City has impounded the dog to remove the risk to local residents and the wider community,” a council spokesperson said in a statement.
sentence: St John WA were called to the scene around 3:40pm on Wednesday, March 4 before she was rushed to Perth Children’s Hospital.
context: St John WA were CT the scene around 3:40pm on Wednesday , March 4 before she was rushed to Perth Children ’s Hospital .

title: Man allegedly armed with bike chain charged with attempting to strangle stranger
plaintext:
A man who allegedly armed himself with a bike chain and attempted to strangle a stranger in inner Sydney has been charged with attempted murder.

Michael Charles Jarvis, 35, is due to appear in bail court on Friday after being charged over an incident in Surry Hills on Wednesday afternoon.

It’s alleged that Mr Jarvis was armed with a bike chain when he attempted to strangle the 26-year-old on Wentworth St.

The man managed to free himself and was allegedly assaulted by Mr Jarvis, who police say then left the scene.

The 26-year-old was taken to hospital and NSW Police commenced an investigation.

Mr Jarvis was arrested at Kings Cross Police Station on Thursday evening.

He was charged with attempting to strangle/suffocate with intent to murder, assault occasioning actual bodily harm and stalk/intimidate intending to cause fear of physical harm.

He will appear in Bail Division Court on Friday.
sentence: Mr Jarvis was arrested at Kings Cross Police Station on Thursday evening.
context: Mr Jarvis was arrested AK Cross Police Station on Thursday evening .

title: Tonkin Highway northbound closed after man critically injured in crash, commuters warned to avoid area
plaintext:
Peak-hour commuters are being warned of traffic chaos after a driver was critically hurt in a crash — forcing the closure of Tonkin Highway northbound.

The crash was reported after 6am on Friday, with multiple ambulance crews and police officers on the scene.

St John WA said a man was being treated for critical injuries.

Tonkin Highway northbound, spanning between the suburbs of Martin and Maddington, has been closed between Mills Road West and Gosnells Road West.

Commuters are urged to consider exiting the freeway at Corfield Street or Albany Highway to avoid congestion.

Traffic remains heavy and is backed up to Corfield Street in Gosnells.

The circumstances of the crash are not known.
sentence: Traffic remains heavy and is backed up to Corfield Street in Gosnells.
context: TR heavy and is backed up to Corfield Street in Gosnells .

title: Master coach puts locals through their paces
plaintext:
Jarrad Schofield’s breadth of experience as a highly regarded mentor at WAFL and AFL levels carried over to local high students who were last week put through their paces by the 2004 Port Adelaide premiership star.

It was a rare opportunity for the region’s next generation of budding stars to learn from the 51-year-old, who broke into the AFL in 1993 with West Coast before playing 206 games that included stints with Fremantle and Port Adelaide.

Schofield in 2013 turned to coaching in the WAFL for Subiaco and led them to flags in 2014-15 and 2018, before returning to Port Adelaide as an assistant coach from 2019-21.

His session with local youngsters complemented a free instructional clinic he led at Morrison Oval for coaches of all levels across the Goldfields Football League.

For the 2022 AFL season, Schofield joined West Coast as an assistant, as strategy and stoppage coach and two years ago he led the Eagles for seven games when Adam Simpson was sacked.

He was replaced by Andrew McQualter and last September signed a two-year deal in a return to Subiaco as senior coach.

His playing career also included three WAFL premierships for Subiaco (2006-08).

Upcoming activities in the GFL this season include an inaugural eight-week wheelchair competition, and a multicultural football program — hailed as the first of its kind in regional WA.
sentence: It was a rare opportunity for the region’s next generation of budding stars to learn from the 51-year-old, who broke into the AFL in 1993 with West Coast before playing 206 games that included stints with Fremantle and Port Adelaide.
context: It was AR opportunity for the region ’s next generation of budding stars to learn from the 51 - year - old , who broke into the AFL in 1993 with West Coast before playing 206 games that included stints with Fremantle and Port Adelaide .

title: El Jalapeño: Trump suggests ‘El Mencho’ would be ‘tremendous’ leader for Iran — calls him ‘very strong, very tough’
plaintext:
WASHINGTON — Describing the Jalisco New Generation Cartel boss as “exactly what Iran needs right now,” President Trump told reporters Tuesday that late drug lord Nemesio “El Mencho” Oseguera Cervantes would make a “fantastic, possibly incredible” Supreme Leader of Iran, praising the cartel kingpin’s “very powerful management style” and “beautiful instincts.”

“He knows how to run an organization, frankly better than most politicians. He’s tough, he’s smart, he built something from nothing — that’s what I respect,” Trump said, adding that El Mencho had “more energy than Khamenei ever did, and believe me, I knew Khamenei, not well, but I knew him.”

Trump reportedly became interested in El Mencho after a briefing on Iranian leadership succession ran long and he began free-associating about “strong men” he admired, at one point also suggesting Tony Soprano before being reminded the character is fictional.

The State Department has declined to comment, while the Treasury Department, which previously had a US $15 million bounty on El Mencho, described the proposal as “creating some internal tension.”

Iranian officials called the suggestion “an insult,” while several Jalisco cartel members expressed cautious optimism about the benefits package.

It is unclear if Trump knew that El Mencho was killed two weeks ago.
sentence: Trump reportedly became interested in El Mencho after a briefing on Iranian leadership succession ran long and he began free-associating about “strong men” he admired, at one point also suggesting Tony Soprano before being reminded the character is fictional.
context: Trump reportedly became interested in El Mencho after a briefing on Iranian leadership succession RL and he began free - associating about “ strong men ” he admired , at one point also suggesting Tony Soprano before being reminded the character is fictional .

title: Comedian Dalin Oliver escapes Dubai amid Middle East conflict
plaintext:
Comedian Dalin Oliver has made it out of Dubai after being stranded for six days amid the escalating conflict between Iran and Israel and the US.

When traveling to Adelaide Fringe for his comedy show, Oliver got stuck in Dubai during a layover. During his time in Dubai, Dalin documented his experience and gave updates.

In his recent update, the comedian said he managed to get a flight to Sydney in Australia and would later catch another flight to Adelaide for his show in the city.

“It was a movie ... it felt like the amazing race,” he jokingly said in an Instagram video. “My thoughts are with everyone still stuck in Dubai. Good luck with getting back home to your friends and family. Thanks for all the love and support.”
sentence: “My thoughts are with everyone still stuck in Dubai.
context: “ My thoughts are with everyone still stuck ID .

title: Mugabe case postponed again as power outage disrupts court
plaintext:
The case involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and his co-accused Tobias Mathondze was postponed again on Thursday when the Alexandra magistrate’s court was left without electricity.

The matter, which relates to the shooting of a 23-year-old gardener at a Hyde Park home, had been scheduled for a bail application but could not proceed.

Defence lawyer Sinenhlanhla Mnguni said it was the second time this week that proceedings have been disrupted by a power outage at the court.

Mugabe, the son of late Zimbabwean president Robert Mugabe, and Mathondze face charges of attempted murder, defeating the ends of justice, possession of an unlicensed firearm and ammunition, two counts of pointing a firearm and contravention of the Immigration Act.

City Power confirmed to TimesLIVE that the Alexandra court is affected by a power outage.

City Power’s Lindiwe Langa said a technical team had been deployed to investigate the problem.

City Power said parts of Alexandra face power disruptions due to ongoing supply disconnections.

It said power was cut on February 19 after widespread tampering and illegal bridging of meters was detected on 9th Avenue between Rev Sam Buti Street and Richard Baloyi Street, and on 11th Avenue between John Nhlanhla Street and Richard Baloyi Street.

According to the utility, electricity will be suspended until full compliance is achieved in the affected areas.
sentence: The case involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and his co-accused Tobias Mathondze was postponed again on Thursday when the Alexandra magistrate’s court was left without electricity.
context: The case involving Bellarmine Chatunga Mugabe and his co - accused Tobias Mathondze WP again on Thursday when the Alexandra magistrate ’s court was left without electricity .

title: Letoya Makhene returns with family reality series ‘The Makhenes’
plaintext:
Letoya Makhene is set to make her small-screen comeback after she and her family scored a reality series called The Makhenes.

The show, set to premier on Mzansi Magic on April 12, follows the family of talented musicians exploring legacy, love, conflict, healing and growth.

At the centre of the series is Letoya. Her followers were last captivated by her fallout with her ex-wife Lebohang Keswa and her stint on Fake Gobela, and she invites them into a different world as she navigates life as a mother, a public figure and a spiritual healer.

Her journey anchors the series, while the stories of her family, including her father musician Blondie Makhene and her sisters Pauline and Gontse, unfold alongside hers, revealing personal struggles, unresolved tensions and moments of transformation.

“This is not another celebrity reality show,” Letoya said.

“It is raw and deeply honest. We are opening up like never before and showing our family exactly as we are. There is nothing glossy or polished about it. It is real. Real love, real pain and real life. I show up as my true, authentic self at a time when I had lost everything and reached my lowest point. That is where this story begins and that is why so many people will see themselves in it.”
sentence: Her followers were last captivated by her fallout with her ex-wife Lebohang Keswa and her stint on Fake Gobela, and she invites them into a different world as she navigates life as a mother, a public figure and a spiritual healer.
context: Her followers were last captivated by her fallout with her ex - wife Lebohang Keswa and HS on Fake Gobela , and she invites them into a different world as she navigates life as a mother , a public figure and a spiritual healer .

title: Pro-Iran militias in Iraq claim attacks on US targets
plaintext:
Pro-Iran militias in Iraq have claimed responsibility for a wave of attacks on "enemy targets" in the country, as the war in Iran enters its sixth day and hostilities spill across the Middle East.

The Islamic State group said on Thursday it had carried out 29 attacks within 24 hours.

The extremists said dozens of drones and missiles were used in the attacks, without specifying the exact locations of the strikes.

It also warned that all countries supporting military actions against Iran would be considered "legitimate targets".

"The criminal American-Zionist enemy continues to mobilise its allies against the free people in the Islamic Republic of Iran and the region," the statement said.

The group also claimed it shot down a drone belonging to what it described as the "American occupation."

The Islamic Resistance in Iraq, an umbrella group of armed factions allied with Iran, has repeatedly claimed attacks on US military facilities and other targets in Iraq and neighbouring countries.

Iraq shares a 1500-kilometre border with Iran. Tehran holds significant political, economic and military influence in the neighbouring country, including through powerful militias loyal to Iran.

Parts of Iraq's Shi'ite community fear that a weakening of Iran could also reduce their own political influence in Iraq.

Some observers warn developments in neighbouring Iran could trigger new unrest in Iraq.
sentence: It also warned that all countries supporting military actions against Iran would be considered "legitimate targets".
context: It also warned that all countries supporting military AA Iran would be considered " legitimate targets " .

title: Fisherman’s body pulled from water at Lord Howe Island
plaintext:
A fisherman’s body has been pulled from the water off Lord Howe Island, off the NSW coast.

The man’s body was spotted floating offshore near the island, which is about 800km east of Sydney, on Wednesday afternoon.

The 51-year-old man could not be revived by emergency services.

A police spokesperson said triple-0 was called about 5pm Wednesday when the man’s body was seen floating near Clear Place Point, on the island’s eastern coast.

“At the scene, officers located the body of a fisherman in the water near a rock platform where fishing equipment was located.

“The body was retrieved by police and NSW SES, before being taken to Gower Wilson Memorial Hospital.

“The 51-year-old man was unable to be revived.”

NSW Police will oversee the investigation and provide information to the coroner.

The Australian territory is governed by the Lowe Howe Island Board, which reports to the NSW state government.
sentence: The man’s body was spotted floating offshore near the island, which is about 800km east of Sydney, on Wednesday afternoon.
context: The man ’s body was spotted floating offshore near the island , which is about 800 KE of Sydney , on Wednesday afternoon .

title: Major city fire engulfs waterfront businesses
plaintext:
A lack of mains water has forced firefighters to resort to pumping river water to try and extinguish a massive blaze that destroyed waterfront businesses in a city's centre.

Just after midnight on Thursday, emergency crews received multiple calls about a fire engulfing Tamar Marine, a boating supplies business in Trevallyn, central Launceston.

Firefighters found a large portion of the building already on fire with most of the flames coming from the second storey.

The crew of 30 firefighters battled the blaze for two hours before it was brought under control.

However, efforts to fight the fire were hindered by the extremely low levels of mains water at the scene.

Crews had to resort to pumping water from the nearby Tamar River to access enough water to slow the progress of the fire.

During the effort to bring the blaze under control, flames spread to the adjoining business, Almasts Australia, and a slipway warehouse which were both destroyed.

Damages from the fire are expected to reach up to $5 million, Tasmania Police said.

An excavator will be brought in today to fully extinguish the fire but there is no threat to residential properties and nobody was injured, emergency services said.

Tasmania Fire Service investigators will be on scene to begin their examination into what caused the fire.

A large amount of smoke is expected to be visible until about lunchtime but all roads in the area are now open.
sentence: An excavator will be brought in today to fully extinguish the fire but there is no threat to residential properties and nobody was injured, emergency services said.
context: An excavator will be brought IT to fully extinguish the fire but there is no threat to residential properties and nobody was injured , emergency services said .

title: Police release image in hunt for man after woman sexually assaulted in Melbourne
plaintext:
Police have released an image they hope will help them identify a man they believe can assist with their investigation into a horror sexual assault in Melbourne.

A 30-year-old woman was dropped off by a rideshare near her property north of Melbourne’s CBD just before 2am on December 4 last year.

A short time later, she passed out on the nature strip and woke up in an unknown unit on Strathmerton St to find a man sexually assaulting her.

The woman managed to push the man off and flee the unit before calling police.

Detectives are appealing for public assistance in identifying a man they describe as Middle Eastern in appearance, aged between 50 and 60, with dark hair, olive skin, a thin build and no facial hair.

Investigators have released a computer-generated image of a man they hope “may be able to assist with the investigation”.

“It is unclear how the woman was moved from the nature strip to the nearby unit,” a police spokeswoman said on Thursday.

“Investigators have made inquiries at the unit, which appears to be unoccupied and often used by squatters and multiple unknown people.”

The rideshare driver has been identified and ruled out as a suspect.

Anyone who can assist investigators with identifying the man, with CCTV/dashcam vision in the area, or any other information is urged to contact Crime Stoppers on 1800 333 000
sentence: “Investigators have made inquiries at the unit, which appears to be unoccupied and often used by squatters and multiple unknown people.”
context: “ Investigators HM inquiries at the unit , which appears to be unoccupied and often used by squatters and multiple unknown people . ”

title: Türkiye’s ruling party submits bill to ban social media for under-15s
plaintext:
Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan’s ruling AK Party submitted to parliament on Wednesday a draft bill that would ban social media access for children younger than 15.

With the bill, Türkiye is following the example of Australia, several European countries and others which have adopted or are considering similar restrictions, amid mounting concerns over the impact of social media on children’s health and safety.

The bill would require social media providers to set up age‑verification systems and to offer parental control tools to ensure the safety of children in the digital world.

Children older than 15 will be provided with separate safe services, and harmful content will be removed within one hour in emergency situations, according to the bill.

Non-compliance with the legislation could trigger fines of up to 3% of a company’s global revenue, or bandwidth restrictions.

Foreign online game distributors will also face new requirements, incuding age-rating their games.

The AKP’s parliamentary group deputy head, Leyla Sahin Usta, said social media and gaming platforms would have six months to comply with the law.

Türkiye already regulates social media companies heavily and is quick to impose takedowns and access bans. It currently bans access to 1.2-million web pages and social media posts, according to a report by local censorship watchdog IFOD.
sentence: Children older than 15 will be provided with separate safe services, and harmful content will be removed within one hour in emergency situations, according to the bill.
context: Children older than 15 will be provided with separate safe services , and harmful content will be removed within OH in emergency situations , according to the bill .

title: ‘Stage 3 burns and a nine-month coma’: Here is what to expect on ‘Bare: As I Am’ tonight
plaintext:
The show is hosted by former 'Skeem Saam' star Pebetsi Nolo Matlaila.

SABC1’s new docu-reality series, Bare: As I Am, will premiere tonight, 4 March 2026.

The show hosted by former Skeem Saam star Pebetsi Nolo Matlaila will air from this Wednesday at 9pm until 15 April.

Bare: As I Am focuses on social issues affecting South Africans and provides a platform for people to share their experiences.

Matlaila described the show as life-changing and impactful.

“It deals with challenges that people do not commonly bring to the forefront,” she said.

“The series shines light and finds solutions to the problems and offers hope and empowers individuals to be in control of their lives.”

What to expect on ‘Bare: As I Am’ tonight

The show runs for 13 weeks, with each episode tackling a different social issue.

It highlights the struggles of participants while promoting awareness, support and practical solutions.

This week’s episode features Winnie Sibuyi, a hairdresser from Pretoria.

She survived an attack in which her ex-partner set their shack on fire. The attack left her with stage 3 burns and a nine-month coma. She now faces permanent scars and a lengthy recovery.

Future episodes will continue to spotlight personal stories and challenges affecting South Africans.
sentence: She survived an attack in which her ex-partner set their shack on fire.
context: She survived an attack in which HE - partner set their shack on fire .

title: Stealing the ‘golden years’ is despicable
plaintext:
Thousands have reached retirement only to discover their nest eggs were quietly looted.

There can be nothing worse than a person getting to the end of their working life and looking forward to some sort of retirement nest egg, only to find that the amount a lot lower than they thought because the company to whom they had been loyal all those years had simply not paid over the employer’s contribution to the retirement fund.

This has happened to tens of thousands of older people and it is an outrageous stain on the reputation of our country.

And this is not just another political theft by ANC cadres – this involves private companies.

Having said that, there were a number of offending municipalities that have, effectively, stolen from their workers.

It is encouraging to hear that those defaulting towns and cities will be deprived of central government’s contribution to their budget.

That is serious, but it will only make things worse. Why not charge those responsible with theft?

And this is even more applicable to private enterprise which, under agreements to the proposed Labour Relations Amendment Act, is being given a slap on the wrist by being forced to pay interest on outstanding money.

Stealing the “golden years” from anyone is despicable and deserves the harshest punishment.
sentence: Stealing the “golden years” from anyone is despicable and deserves the harshest punishment.
context: ST “ golden years ” from anyone is despicable and deserves the harshest punishment .

title: Making It! 2026: CDI marks 25 years of growing SA’s creative economy
plaintext:
Making It! 2026 celebrates 25 years of the Craft and Design Institute (CDI) shaping South Africa’s creative economy. Taking place from March 24 to 25 in the Kramerville Design District, the conference brings together designers, founders, strategists and investors to focus on one key theme: scaling creative businesses.

The conference will be hosted at Level Three in Sandton, with an evening reception at Katy’s Palace Bar.

Founded in 2001, the CDI has grown from supporting 63 makers to a national network of more than 8,300 creative enterprises across all nine provinces. Making It! reflects the sector’s shift from participation to structured growth, national presence and long-term sustainability.

Award-winning broadcaster Shado Twala will serve as MC, leading a powerful line-up that includes ceramic artist Andile Dyalvane, Jozi My Jozi’s Dawn Robertson, Chocolate Tribe CEO Nosipho Maketo-van den Bragt, CDI founding CEO Erica Elk, BASA CEO Beth Arendse, cultural worker Thulile Gamedze, and design entrepreneur Joey Khuvutlu.

The programme also features voices from fintech, marketing and architecture, including Dave Duarte of Treeshake, Ronald Makomba of Yoco**, and Prof Emmanuel Nkambule from the University of Johannesburg.
sentence: Making It! 2026 celebrates 25 years of the Craft and Design Institute (CDI) shaping South Africa’s creative economy.
context: Making It ! 2026 celebrates 25 years of the Craft and Design Institute ( CDI ) SS Africa ’s creative economy .

title: Facebook down: Global outage hits social media giant, Instagram users report issues with Meta
plaintext:
Social media giant Meta is suffering a global outage, with users worldwide reporting they are unable to access Facebook and Instragram.

In Australia, popular outage website Down Detector recorded thousands of users saying they were unable to access Facebook. The number of recorder users unable to access the platform was higher in the UK and US.

Results were similar for Meta owned Instagram.

When users attempt to log in or access the site, they are met with a message stating their account is “temporarily unavailable”.

Meta’s business suite has also reported issues for WhatsApp.

The company has not yet commented on the global outage.

More to come...
sentence: Results were similar for Meta owned Instagram.
context: Results were similar for MO Instagram .

title: Former Nomura Securities Employee Given 18-year Prison Sentence for Arson, Attempted Murder-Robbery in Hiroshima
plaintext:
HIROSHIMA — The Hiroshima district court handed down an 18-year prison sentence to a former employee of Nomura Securities Co. for arson and attempted murder-robbery of an elderly woman.

According to the ruling, Yusei Kajiwara, 30, gave the woman sleeping pills at her home in Nishi Ward, Hiroshima City, and stole about ¥18 million on July 28, 2024. He then set fire to the house.

The woman and her husband were his customers.

During the court proceedings, the prosecution and the defense argued over whether the arson constituted attempted murder. Prosecutors claimed there were flammable objects, such as a bookshelf, around where Kajiwara started the fire.

“The act was extremely dangerous and could have easily resulted in her death,” the prosecutors said.

The defense, however, demanded that the court dismiss the charge of attempted murder-robbery, saying, “He only attempted to burn the closet where the bag containing the cash was, and didn’t intend to kill her.”
sentence: The defense, however, demanded that the court dismiss the charge of attempted murder-robbery, saying, “He only attempted to burn the closet where the bag containing the cash was, and didn’t intend to kill her.”
context: The defense , however , DT the court dismiss the charge of attempted murder - robbery , saying , “ He only attempted to burn the closet where the bag containing the cash was , and did n’t intend to kill her . ”

title: Letters to the Editor: We could learn a lot from Neil Sedaka’s underrated ‘The Immigrant’
plaintext:
To the editor: I was saddened by the news of Neil Sedaka’s death, but even more so by the tributes and obituaries in which his greatest song (in my opinion) was not mentioned (“Neil Sedaka, songwriter and hitmaker over multiple generations, dies at 86,” Feb. 27). “The Immigrant,” released in 1974, opens with these lyrics:

Harbors opened their arms to the young searching foreignerCome to live in the light of the beacon of liberty.

Wow, have times changed. Now we’re the beacon of corruption, greed and hate.

I think we’d do well to remember Sedaka’s chorus too:

There was a time when strangers were welcome here.Music would play, they tell me the days were sweet and clear.It was a sweeter tune and there was so much room that people could come from everywhere.

The tune was as inspiring as his words. RIP Neil Sedaka and the American dream.

Liz White, Los Angeles
sentence: Music would play, they tell me the days were sweet and clear.
context: Music would play , they tell me the days WS and clear .

title: ‘So happy, so in love’: Mandla N pays lobola for Melissa Nayimuli
plaintext:
The couple announced their engagement in December last year.

Producer and director Mandla N has paid lobola for Miss Universe South Africa 2025 Melissa Nayimuli.

This comes after the Black Brain Pictures boss proposed in 2025.

Nayimuli shared highlights from their lobola ceremony day on Instagram. She posted a photo of herself in a custom dress by fashion designer Stephen van Eeden.

She also shared videos and photos featuring her husband and family, giving fans a glimpse of the celebration.

“We are still feeling so overwhelmed with the love and support we’ve received from our families. What a blessed day! So happy, so in love.

“To my friend, the artistic genius that is [Stephen van Eeden], you made me feel and look like an African queen in this dress! Definitely one of the best gifts I’ve ever received. Forever grateful to you,” she wrote.

Mandla N and Melissa’s engagement

Mandla N proposed to Nayimuli during a private island getaway in Thailand.

The proposal took place at the Garrya Tongsai Bay Samui shortly after her return from the Miss Universe stage on 21 November 2025.

The couple shared the engagement video on Instagram. “Our favourite scene yet: Saying YES!!!!” Nayimuli wrote.
sentence: “Our favourite scene yet: Saying YES!!!!”
context: “ OF scene yet : Saying YES ! ! ! ! ”

title: Stellantis’s innovation chief says patent filings will stay high despite strategic reset
plaintext:
Car giant Stellantis plans to keep a high level of patent filings despite the cancellation of many electric vehicle (EV) projects as part of a strategic reset, its chief of innovation Anne Laliron said on Tuesday.

“CEO Antonio Filosa is very much in favour of promoting creativity and protecting our innovations. We have very strong support for innovation and patents,” she told reporters.

The company filed 1,294 patents in France last year, half of its worldwide total, she added.

Stellantis last month announced a €22.2bn (R422.03bn) writedown as it scales back its EV ambitions in the face of a slower-than-expected market.

Reuters
sentence: “CEO Antonio Filosa is very much in favour of promoting creativity and protecting our innovations.
context: “ CEO Antonio Filosa is very much in favour of promoting creativity and protecting OI .
